title: 9 Herbal Creams For Ankylosing Spondylitis
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan for ankylosing spondylitis, a type of arthritis that causes inflammation in the spine and joints. These creams often contain herbs that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and stiffness.  For example, Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects. When applied topically in a cream form, it can help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected joints.  Another example is Zingiber officinale, or ginger, which has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including arthritis. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and stiffness, and it can also help improve circulation, which is essential for healing and reducing inflammation.  Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, is an herb that is commonly used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including arthritis. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and stiffness, and it can also help improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by ankylosing spondylitis.  Using herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help reduce your reliance on pain medications, which can have side effects. Additionally, herbal creams can be a more natural and gentle way to manage your symptoms, which can be especially important if you're looking for a more holistic approach to your health. By incorporating herbal creams into your treatment plan, you may be able to reduce your pain and stiffness, improve your mobility, and overall improve your quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains the bioactive constituents curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, such as COX-2 and TNF-alpha, which contribute to the progression of the disease. The antioxidant properties of curcumin also help protect the joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis and promote joint health.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Curcuma longa powder in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Add the Curcuma longa powder to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before using as a topical cream for ankylosing spondylitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and pain in the joints, making them useful for managing ankylosing spondylitis. Gingerols also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Zingiber officinale creams may also help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the spine and joints. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of ginger essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add the shea butter to the melted oil and wax mixture. Stir until the shea butter is fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Stir in the ginger essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Zingiber officinale cream is ready to use for ankylosing spondylitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams contain bioactive constituents such as withanolides, withaferin A, and ashwagandhans A and B. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the joints associated with ankylosing spondylitis. The cream's anti-oxidant properties also help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to disease progression. Additionally, the cream's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system, which can become overactive in ankylosing spondylitis, leading to inflammation and damage to the joints. Regular application of Withania somnifera creams may help alleviate symptoms and slow disease progression by targeting the underlying inflammatory and oxidative mechanisms.
remedies.image_alt: Withania somnifera herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Withania somnifera powder, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Add the Withania somnifera powder and vitamin E oil, and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden completely. Once hardened, your Withania somnifera cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhetic acid and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation in the body. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the joints and muscles, which is beneficial for people with ankylosing spondylitis. Glycyrrhetic acid blocks the production of certain chemicals that cause inflammation, while flavonoids neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues. The anti-inflammatory effects of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help manage symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, and 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Add beeswax and glycerin to the melted mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add distilled water and Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let it cool and solidify. Once set, use a spatula to scoop and apply to affected areas as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains active constituents like rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, and carnosic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the joints and muscles, a common issue in ankylosing spondylitis. The antioxidant properties of Salvia officinalis also protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the progression of the disease. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Salvia officinalis creams may help reduce pain and stiffness in the spine and joints, making it a potential natural remedy for ankylosing spondylitis. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Salvia officinalis creams may also help slow down the progression of the disease by reducing the severity of inflammation in the body.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Heat the carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Steep the dried Salvia officinalis leaves in the hot oil for 2-3 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Combine 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax and shea butter are melted and the mixture is smooth. Pour into a container and let cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata creams contains boswellic acids, including acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA), acetyl-β-boswellic acid (ABB), and 11-keto-β-boswellic acid (KBA). These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and joint pain in people with ankylosing spondylitis. Boswellic acids block the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, specifically 5-lipoxygenase, which contributes to the development of inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation and joint pain, Boswellia serrata creams may help improve mobility and quality of life for individuals with ankylosing spondylitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of boswellic acids also help reduce the progression of ankylosing spondylitis by preventing bone fusion.
remedies.image_alt: Boswellia serrata herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of frankincense oil, and 10 drops of boswellia essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in the shea butter until it's fully incorporated.
Add the frankincense oil and boswellia essential oil, and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar, let it cool and solidify, then use as needed for ankylosing spondylitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are active constituents that help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba also have anti-edematous effects, which can help reduce swelling and promote joint mobility. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids can improve nitric oxide levels, which helps relax blood vessels and promote circulation to the affected areas. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba creams may provide relief from the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, and 2 teaspoons of Ginkgo biloba extract.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and stir in vitamin E oil and Ginkgo biloba extract until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden before use as a cream for ankylosing spondylitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains sesquiterpene lactones, particularly achillin, and flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Achillea millefolium also contains phenolic acids, including ferulic acid, that can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms, such as stiffness and swelling, commonly experienced by individuals with ankylosing spondylitis. Regular application of Achillea millefolium creams may provide relief from these symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a saucepan and heat it over low heat (100°F to 150°F) for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the glass jar, pressing on the solids to get as much oil as possible.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the oil mixture and heat it over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken. Pour it into small containers and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains a compound called linalool, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling and pain in the joints, a common symptom of ankylosing spondylitis. Linalyl acetate, another active constituent in the cream, has analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help to ease muscle tension and stiffness. The antioxidant properties of these compounds also help to protect the joints from further damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and easing pain, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help to improve the quality of life for people with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for ankylosing spondylitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Combine the distilled water and dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers in a saucepan. Heat over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate saucepan, melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Combine the oil and beeswax mixture with the Lavandula angustifolia infusion. Stir until well combined. Pour into a container and let cool.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce inflammation and joint pain, but it's unlikely to prevent ankylosing spondylitis from forming. Some herbal teas, like ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help manage symptoms, but they don't address the underlying causes of the condition.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your ankylosing spondylitis treatment, but it's essential to consume them in moderation. Drinking herbal teas every day can interact with medications or worsen symptoms, especially if you're sensitive to certain herbs. Start with small amounts and observe how your body reacts before making it a daily habit.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help manage ankylosing spondylitis symptoms, but it's hard to say exactly when results will be seen. Some people report relief from pain and stiffness within a few days to a week after starting to drink herbal teas. Others may notice improvement in a few weeks, but it can take several months to feel a significant difference.
faq_time: For ankylosing spondylitis, drinking herbal tea in the morning can help ease stiffness and discomfort throughout the day. This is because the tea can start working early on to reduce inflammation and promote flexibility in your joints and spine, making it a good habit to get into right after waking up.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for ankylosing spondylitis. For example, some teas can slow down blood clotting, which may increase the risk of bleeding when taken with blood thinners used to treat this condition. This interaction can be serious and may require changes to medication.
faq_pregnant: If you have ankylosing spondylitis and are pregnant, it's a good idea to talk to your healthcare provider about herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like those containing ginger or turmeric, might help with pain. However, others like willow bark, evening primrose, or blue cohosh may have risks for you and your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for ankylosing spondylitis may seem like a good idea, but it's not clear how safe they are while breastfeeding. Some herbs, like feverfew and willow bark, can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. It's best to avoid them or try a different remedy to ease symptoms. Always check the ingredients and ask the tea maker.
title_2: HLA-B27 and Ankylosing Spondylitis: 9 Creams to Manage Symptoms
